other_observers: None on Nov 16.  A first-year GWGU, probably the same bird, was seen by observers at other locations in Colorado between mid December and late February.
description: The first-year Glaucous-winged Gull was seen resting on a sand bar, bathing in the nearby shallow water, and flying low over the lake.  It was approximately the same size as the nearby Herring Gulls but had a shorter primary projection.  The gull had a stout appearance.  Plumage was rather uniformly biscuit colored.  Some fine, low-contrast patterning could be seen on the upperparts.  Some streaking could be seen on the neck, and the head was somewhat paler than the rest of the upperparts.  The folded primaries were the same color as the back, with paler edges.  Tertials were plain (not patterned).  Wing color also appeared uniform when the bird spread its wings in the water and in flight.  A light brown tail band could be seen on the bird in flight.  The bill was all black and very thick.  Legs were pink.
photographer: Photographer: David Dowell, David Dowell, David Dowell, David Dowell, David Dowell
